data.title: Rio Tinto reportedly seeking bailout for Australian aluminum smelter
data.description:
Australia’s Tomago aluminum smelter, majority-owned by global mining giant Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO), is in discussions with state and government officials to secure a multibillion-dollar bailout package to offset rising energy costs, The Australian Financial Review reported.

Negotiations have focused on the smelter’s 2026–2029 electricity contract and the structure of the federal government’s production tax credits, sources familiar with the talks said on the condition of anonymity, as they are not authorized to issue public comments.

“They’re running around in Sydney and Canberra looking for a lot of money,” a source said, adding, “It’s billions, not hundreds of millions. It’s eye-watering.”

Located in the state of New South Wales, Tomago, as Australia’s biggest aluminum producer, accounts for roughly 37% of the country’s primary aluminum output and consumes about 10% of NSW’s power supply.

Negotiations are predominantly being conducted at the state government level, with Rio (NYSE:RIO) directly approaching Premier Chris Minns, another source said, adding that it is likely the final deal will be “more sophisticated” than a direct subsidy.

Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO), which has a 51.6% stake in Tomago, said in February that the company will decide on the facility within six months, as power price contracts issued to date have made its future unviable beyond 2028.

“The problem we are facing with a place like Tomago right now is it might take us a longer time to get renewables, and the electricity from coal-fired power, what we have been quoted, is extremely expensive,” noted RIO boss Jakob Stausholm.

Australia’s aluminum producers, including Rio Tinto (RIO) and Alcoa (AA), stand to benefit from the ruling Labor government’s newly launched A$2B ($1.3B) worth of Green Aluminium Production Credit, available as an incentive to switch their smelters to renewable power before 2036.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: Boeing, Apple and Visa among top picks
data.description:
The S&P500 (SP500) closed in the green, as the market this week witnessed a strong jobs report and an escalating war of words between U.S. President Donald Trump and Tesla CEO Elon Musk, signalling an end of their bromance era.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) rose 0.3%, while Dow (DJI) showed marginal gains.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Boeing upgraded on trade support, defense boost

Boeing (NYSE:BA) was upgraded to Buy from Neutral by BofA, pointing to a strengthening production momentum, improving free cash flow prospects and the aircraft maker’s growing role in global trade negotiations.

BofA analyst Ron Epstein highlighted Boeing’s inclusion in recent trade deals as a strategic asset for the Trump administration, citing aircraft orders tied to agreements with the United Kingdom, Qatar, UAE and the recent lifting of China’s ban on Boeing jets.

Epstein added, this "favored trade mechanism" status may set a precedent for Boeing to benefit from future international negotiations.

Apple gets rare rating cut from Needham

Needham downgraded Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) to Hold from Buy amid concerns over earnings growth, iPhone competition and more.

“Despite AAPL's premium valuation, it is growing [revenues] and margins slowest among its Big Tech competitors,” analyst Laura Martin said, expressing concern about Apple's valuation.

With an iPhone replacement cycle not likely to happen in the next 12 months, revenue growth could be at risk, added Martin, noting that $170 to $180 is a better entry level for investors.

JPM upgrades Pinterest on user growth,

J.P. Morgan upgraded Pinterest (NYSE:PINS) to Overweight from Neutral, praising the image-sharing social media platform for growing its users, improving its monetization and ARPU, and driving profitable growth. The brokerage hiked PT to $40 from $35.

"Importantly, we believe PINS is leveraging its full-funnel ad approach and automation/AI capabilities — including Performance+—to capture a greater share of ad spending," JPM said, adding that the company has seen "solid MAU growth" and JPM expects upside to multi-year profit from faster revenue growth and cost discipline for PINS.

Constellation Energy downgraded after Meta nuclear deal

Citi downgraded Constellation Energy (NASDAQ:CEG) shares to Neutral from Buy, citing limited near-term upside following the stock’s recent rally and news of its power purchase agreement with Meta.

"This deal has broad implications on the power markets as it signals future contracting activity," Levine wrote, but he believes much of the benefit from such agreements is priced into Constellation's stock price, which has surged since Citi's upgrade in April.

The brokerage sees a “balanced risk to the upside and the downside” for the stock, maintaining a High Risk classification due to volatility in power prices, policy uncertainty, and execution challenges related to plant restarts.

Bank of America stayed bullish on Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) with a Buy rating and is still optimistic about the company's unique and valuable assets, despite noting that WBD shares have significantly underperformed its expectations and the broader market.

Mizuho upgraded Visa (NYSE:V) to Outperform from Neutral on the premise that the U.S. consumers will increase their spending in categories where they're more likely to pay with a card than cash.

Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) was in focus as BofA maintained its Buy rating after a meeting with management. Analyst Vivek Arya said the tone of the meeting with Nvidia CFO Colette Kress and an investor relations executive was “very positive,” as demand and customer interest across both the enterprise and cloud remain strong.

Bank of America named Chewy (NYSE:CHWY) as its top Internet SMID cap idea. The firm pointed to consistent share gains against the pet industry, exposure to non-discretionary and subscription-like sales, as well as margin expansion amid the online retailer's mix shift into pet health and sponsored ads. Jefferies also downgraded the stock to Hold from Buy, citing valuation concerns and that the company is unlikely to materially raise full-year guidance when it reports Q1 results next week.

data.title: Texas measles cases may have peaked, official says
data.description:
The Texas Department of State Health Services has recorded no additional cases of measles in the state since its June 3 update, while a key agency official said that the outbreak that began early this year is “on the decline.”

Since late January, the Lone Star State has reported 742 measles cases, including 94 hospitalizations and two fatalities, as the outbreak primarily concentrated in West Texas continued to spread.

As case numbers continue to drop, the DSHS said it will issue measles updates on a weekly basis starting from Tuesday, June 10.  

“We appear to be at the downward end of this curve for this particular outbreak in West Texas,” Texas Standard reported, quoting DSHS commissioner Dr. Jennifer Shuford, who noted that under-vaccinated communities are particularly vulnerable to the infection.

“We know that there are lots of measles transmitting all over the United States and around the world. And so it just takes one person to get into an under-vaccinated community to cause another outbreak,” Shuford said.

“Thankfully, this large outbreak that we’ve been tracking in west Texas does seem to be on the decline,” she added. MMR vaccines are considered the most effective way to contain the measles spread. Merck (NYSE:MRK) and GSK (NYSE:GSK) market MMR vaccines in the U.S.

data.title: Apple WWDC preview: A 'low-key' event on the horizon
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is set to host its annual Worldwide Developers Conference next week and while last year's event focused on the promise of Apple Intelligence, this year's event may be more geared on a back-to-basics approach.

Goldman Sachs analyst Michael Ng recently said that the keynote address is likely to focus on design improvements to its various operating systems, some information about its progress in artificial intelligence and other less sizable announcements than in years past.

“We expect AAPL to demonstrate continued progress toward incorporating AI into its operating systems through developer access to its AI models for app development … as well as the potential announcement of Gemini AI integration on iPhones given Alphabet CEO Sundar Pichai’s recent comments regarding hopes to finalize a partnership with Apple by mid-2025,” Ng said.

He's also expecting the next version of iOS to include a number of convenience-oriented features, including public Wi-Fi access syncing, AI-powered battery optimization and more.

Rosenblatt Securities analyst Barton Crockett said the event is likely to be “low-key,” with expectations lower than they have been previously, with minor updates likely to get the majority of the attention.

Last month, it was reported that Apple is set to rebrand its various operating systems for the purpose of providing more consistency and removing confusion from developers and customers. 

The company could also open up its AI models to third-party developers in a strategy to increase app production, according to previous media reports.

Some other new AI features expected to be announced include: live translations for phone calls, text messages and for AirPods; a new Shortcuts app and third-party large language model support for Xcode, Bloomberg reported.

Evercore ISI analyst Amit Daryanani had a similar take to Ng in that the upcoming WWDC is likely to be a “transition year.” 

“Expectations are rightfully tempered this year at WWDC relative to prior years where we had more material announcements — 2024 (Apple Intelligence) and 2023 (Vision Pro) events,” Daryanani wrote in a note to clients. “The largest announcement will likely be Apple opening its foundation models to app developers so that they can be leveraged for on device AI. In addition, AAPL may announce more partner options when it comes to their current Apple Intelligence partners beyond OpenAI that will be integrated into the iOS offering natively – Perplexity and Gemini could be logical choices.”

“There will also likely be some other minor AI announcements, but nothing that will change the popular narrative that Apple is behind on AI,” Daryanani added. 

Apple's WWDC is set to run between June 9 and June 13, with the keynote address set for June 9 at 1 p.m. EST.

data.title: Eli Lilly is said to have posted 60% rise in obesity drug sales in India last month
data.description:
Sales of Eli Lilly’s (NYSE:LLY) Mounjaro in India rose 60% in May from April, the first full month the popular weight loss/diabetes drug was available in the country following its commercial rollout in late March, Bloomberg News reported on Saturday.

Citing data from India-based market analysis firm Pharmarack Technologies, the publication said Lilly (NYSE:LLY) recorded ₹126M ($1.5M) worth of Mounjaro sales last month. That figure included ₹75M sales of the drug’s 5mg version, indicating over a twofold rise, and ₹51M sales of its 2.5mg version.

“The patient number may have actually doubled” over the period, noted Pharmarack Vice President Sheetal Sapale, and the demand for the GLP-1 drug should continue to rise with the 5mg version being prescribed to patients after four weeks on the 2.5mg starting dose.

The Indiana-based drugmaker gained a first-mover advantage in India’s fast-expanding obesity market when it launched Mounjaro 2.5 mg and 5 mg vials at 3,500 rupees ($40.50) and 4,375 rupees ($50.63), respectively, early this year.

Its rival Novo Nordisk (NVO) has yet to roll out its popular weight loss therapy semaglutide in India, where nearly 100M adults live with obesity.

data.title: EchoStar prepares potential bankruptcy filing - WSJ
data.description:
EchoStar (NASDAQ:SATS) -14.2% post-market Friday after The Wall Street Journal reported the company is considering a Chapter 11 bankruptcy filing, as it faces a Federal Communications Commission review of certain of its wireless and satellite spectrum rights.

EchoStar (NASDAQ:SATS) has skipped ~$500M in debt interest payments in recent days, starting a countdown that would push the company into default before July if not cured within the bonds' grace period, according to the report.

The company said in securities filings that the FCC's threats had "effectively frozen our ability to make decisions" about how to grow and invest in its Boost Mobile business.

Some analysts believe a bankruptcy filing could offer EchoStar (SATS) some protection from potential regulatory actions, although the outcome of such a court process is not clear, the WSJ report said.

data.title: Crude oil posts strongest weekly gain this year on renewed U.S.-China optimism
data.description:
Crude oil futures wrapped up their best weekly showing so far this year, lifted by optimism that renewed engagement this week between the U.S. and China could lead to trade agreements.

The resumption of U.S.-China trade talks and news that President Trump and China's President Xi spoke on the phone and agreed to meet soon helped ease concerns about an escalating tariff conflict that could significantly hurt oil demand.

The trade talks, combined with the lack of progress toward a Russia-Ukraine ceasefire or a U.S.-Iran nuclear deal, outweighed concerns about accelerating output increases by OPEC+, which analysts believe will be largely absorbed in the near term by summer demand.

Oil rose nearly 2% on Friday after U.S. May payrolls came in higher than expected, with employers adding 139K jobs, prompting Price Futures Group's Phil Flynn to call the report "Goldilocks... not too hot, not too cold but just right to increase the chances for an interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve."

Still, expectations that OPEC+ will agree to further large production hikes to regain market share and take advantage of seasonal demand continue to hang over the oil market.

Analysts at HSBC expect OPEC+ will agree to two large output hikes for August and September at their next meetings, likely implementiing increases of 411K bbl/day and 274K bbl/day, respectively, during the periods, thus raising downside risks to the bank's $65/bbl Brent forecast from Q4 2025.

The bank sees the oil market as balanced in Q2 and Q3 as demand rises in the summer and peaks in July-August, matching the OPEC+ supply increases, but accelerated hikes after the end of summer would risk tipping the market into a bigger Q4 surplus than previously forecast.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for July delivery finished +6.2% to $64.58/bbl this week, its largest one-week percentage gain since November 2024, and front-month August Brent crude (CO1:COM) closed +5.9% for the week to $66.47, its best one-week percentage gain since October; on Friday, WTI and Brent gained 1.9% and 1.7%, respectively.

U.S. natural gas futures ended the day and the week higher, as prospects of strong summer demand outweighed this week's large storage build and decline in LNG exports due to maintenance at several terminals; front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for July delivery settled +9.8% to $3.784/MMBtu this week; on Friday, gas gained 2.9%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

YTD, Nymex crude has declined 9.9% and Brent has dropped 10.9%, but U.S. natural gas is up 4.1%.

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), finished the week +2.3%.

Top 15 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Top Ships (TOPS) +43.6%, U.S. Antimony (UAMY) +39.2%, Taseko Mines (TGB) +37.2%, NET Power (NPWR) +37.1%, Brooge Holdings (BROG) +36.4%, First Majestic Silver (AG) +34%, Foremost Clean Energy (FMST) +33.9%, USA Rare Earth (USAR) +29%, FuelCell Energy (FCEL) +26.6%, Perpetua Resources (PPRT) +24.8%, Endeavour Silver (EXK) +24.4%, ASP Isotopes (ASPI) +23.8%, Dakota Gold (DC) +22.2%, Hecla Mining (HL) +22%, Comstock (LODE) +20.9%.

Top 5 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: PrimeEnergy (PNRG) -25.4%, Sable Offshore (SOC) -14.8%, Zeo Energy (ZEO) -11.9%, Opal Fuels (OPAL) -9.6%, DRDGold (DRD) -8.4%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: UroGen closes up 43% ahead of FDA decision on bladder cancer candidate
data.description:
• UroGen Pharma (NASDAQ:URGN) closed Friday up ~43%, a week before the U.S. FDA is set to act on its NDA for UGN-102 (mitomycin), a treatment for a type of bladder cancer.
  • It is not immediately clear what caused the surge today.
  • UGN-102 has a June 13 PDUFA date for recurrent low-grade intermediate-risk non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.
  • Last month, shares dropped precipitously after an agency advisory committee voted 4-5 that the benefits of the therapy outweigh its risks.

data.title: Eni, YPF sign deal to develop Argentina LNG project
data.description:
Italy's Eni (NYSE:E) and Argentina's YPF (NYSE:YPF) said Friday they signed an agreement to develop the Argentina liquefied natural gas export project, underpinned by natural gas from the Vaca Muerta shale formation.

The Argentina LNG project is designed to export up to 30M tons/year of LNG by 2030, using a phased approach.

The agreement, which builds on a memorandum of understanding signed in April, is a step toward a final investment decision that could be finalized by year-end, YPF (NYSE:YPF) CEO Horacio Marín said.

Eni (NYSE:E) said it was selected as a strategic partner for Argentina LNG "due to its specific and distinctive know-how developed during its FLNG projects in Congo and Mozambique and its global leadership in the construction of projects with similar technological requirements."

The project would transport natural gas from the Neuquen basin to a coastal site in Rio Negro, where it will be liquefied using floating liquefaction vessels for overseas export; Eni (E) said it would initially provide two FLNG units with a combined capacity of 12M tons/year.

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court grants DOGE access to social security data
data.description:
The U.S. Supreme Court on Friday allowed the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) access to the social security information of millions of Americans by lifting a restriction placed earlier by a lower district court. 

DOGE was set up under the leadership of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) chief executive Elon Musk to cut federal spending. Musk exited the agency last week, and has since then spectacularly fallen out with U.S. President Donald Trump in a public verbal spat.

When DOGE was established in January by Trump, his executive order required other government agencies to allow employees "full and prompt access to all unclassified agency records, software systems, and IT systems." But U.S. District Judge Ellen Hollander in April ordered the Social Security Administration (SSA) from granting DOGE affiliates access to various records. 

The Trump administration appealed to the 4th U.S. Circuit Court of Appeals to stay Hollander's order, but lost that request by a vote of 9-6. It then turned to the Supreme Court.  

"When considering whether to grant a stay, this Court looks to four factors: '(1) whether the stay applicant has made a strong showing that he is likely to succeed on the merits; (2) whether the applicant will be irreparably injured absent a stay; (3) whether issuance of the stay will substantially injure the other parties interested in the proceeding; and (4) where the public interest lies,'" the Supreme Court's unsigned order said. 

"After review, we determine that the application of these factors in this case warrants granting the requested stay. We conclude that, under the present circumstances, SSA may proceed to afford members of the SSA DOGE Team access to the agency records in question in order for those members to do their work," the order added.

There were three dissenting opinions among the Supreme Court judges, including Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson.

"The Government wants to give DOGE unfettered access to this personal, non-anonymized information right now—before the courts have time to assess whether DOGE’s access is lawful. So it asks this Court to stay a lower court’s decision to place temporary and qualified limits on DOGE’s data access while litigation challenging DOGE’s authority to access the data is pending," Jackson wrote.

"But the Government fails to substantiate its stay request by showing that it or the public will suffer irreparable harm absent this Court’s intervention. In essence, the 'urgency' underlying the Government’s stay application is the mere fact that it cannot be bothered to wait for the litigation process to play out before proceeding as it wishes," Jackson added. 

Wall Street has largely ignored the noise around DOGE and Elon Musk, though his verbal spat with Trump did weigh on investor sentiment for Tesla's (TSLA) shares, with the stock eventually shedding more than $150B in market cap.

Here are some exchange-traded funds that track the benchmark S&P 500 index (SP500):  (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).
